Demo for review. Uses sample PC27 data. No live launch actions have been approved.

Practicing the Way ticketing

PC27 registration demo

A working demo and a branded preview, ready for review.

Event

Pastor Conference 2027, Bridgetown Church, Portland

Scope

Sample invitation-only registration flow

Boundary

Demo only. Sample data. No live payments, site changes, or email sends.

Where we are

The demo is ready for screen share. The path to launch still needs owners.

ReadyWorking demo event

A demo PC27 event is set up and running: pricing, checkout, tickets, and check-in are all functional.

ReadyBranded preview

A custom-styled version shows the PTW visual direction: matching typeface, green and cream palette, clean square design.

ReadyDemo setup

The demo is running and ready for screen share.

OpenLaunch decisions

Owners, registration model, pricing, payment processing, email, website, and support still need decisions.

What we know

PC27 is an invitation workflow, with ticketing as the registration layer.

Based on what we know so far: lead-pastor invitations, team-member limits, non-refundable tickets, and wave-based pricing. The demo should help the team decide how these pieces work together.

Known
April 12 to 14, 2027 at Bridgetown Church in Portland
Known
Invitation-only, lead-pastor centered attendance model
Draft
$800 standard and $700 early price are examples, not final prices
Open
Application review, invite-only registration, or invite code to checkout
Open
Owners for website, domain, email, payments, support, and check-in

Demo walkthrough

The attendee experience in four steps.

01
Invitation landing

Show the conference context, eligibility details, dates, venue, and pricing.

02
Registration form

Collect buyer, church, lead pastor, attendee role, and non-refundable acknowledgement.

03
Payment and ticket

Walk through the demo checkout, apply the early-bird code, then open the confirmation and QR ticket screens.

04
Door and support

Show name search, scan behavior, duplicate warning, ticket resend, and escalation needs.

Working demo

The hosted demo shows the full registration workflow.

EventA demo PC27 event is live and running online.
Codeearly700 changes the demo ticket from $800 to $700.
CheckoutA test checkout has been completed with demo payment data.
DoorCheck-in supports search by name, order number, attendee number, or email.
Hosted demo. This version uses the platform's default styling; the branded version is shown on the next slide.
Local branded preview connected to the sample event.

Visual direction

This is what a PTW-branded registration page can look and feel like.

The design matches PTW: the same typeface (Satoshi), green and cream palette, square corners, and a clean, quiet surface.

Available for screen-share review. The hosted demo does not yet show this branded styling.

Font licensing and platform attribution terms need confirmation before launch.

What the demo covers

The demo covers the key parts of the attendee experience.

BuyerProduct, promo, form, payment

Demo ticket, early-tier code, buyer questions, attendee questions, non-refundable acknowledgement.

TicketEmail and QR delivery

Order confirmation and attendee ticket emails are set up. The QR ticket page is working with demo data.

OperatorCheck-in and support

Check-in list, name lookup, duplicate-scan warning, and support steps are ready to walk through.

This is a demo environment. Launch setup is a separate step.

Door and support

Check-in works. The open question is staffing and day-of operations.

The platform handles QR scan and name lookup at the door. The question is whether a standard setup with training is enough, or whether the event needs a custom check-in view for specific attendance rules.

Ticket screen for visual review. Staff check-in links stay private.

Decisions we need

Six questions to answer from this demo.

FlowApplication or registration

Approval before purchase, invite code to checkout, or a registration page with manual review.

PriceCodes and limits

Final ticket price, early-tier discount, per-church team limits, and response windows.

OwnersAccess and approvals

Website, domain, email sender, payment processing, support, door check-in, and final go-live approval.

PaymentsPayment terms

Who holds the payment account, fee structure, refund policy, and dispute handling.

OpsSupport model

Ticket resend, attendee edits, refund exceptions, failed email, and day-of escalation paths.

PreviewShareable link

Whether the team needs a shareable branded demo link, or screen-share review is enough.

Recommended close

End with a clear next sequence.

If the direction looks right, the next step is collecting owners and building a setup plan for each system. Design approval, operational decisions, and launch are separate steps.

1Confirm registration model and ticket rules.
2Collect owner names and access paths.
3Decide whether to create a shareable branded demo link.
4Build setup plans for each system: website, payments, email, and domain.
5Run a final review pass only after real event details and owners are confirmed.
